formulation_count: ~62 PLGA 50:50 data points plus 9-row sets for PLGA 65:35/75:25/85:15 from one source paper
paper_count: 8 PLGA antiviral papers after screening from 275 initial publications; some ratio-specific sets from single paper
molecule_count: antiviral small molecules/prodrugs; not broad enough for cross-drug descriptor learning
preparation_scope: PLGA antiviral nanoparticle literature; not a single controlled method scope
input_features_reported: particle size; PDI; drug load/DL%; EE%; PLGA ratio grouping; limited formulation/process variables discussed narratively
targets: EE%; DL%; size/PDI used for Gaussian Process visualization
validation_strategy: descriptive/predictive visualization; no strong grouped external validation; limited data regions explicitly noted as unreliable
interpretability: visual GP surfaces; qualitative trend discussion
key_limitations: small and sparse dataset; output variables dominate inputs; limited formulation/process predictors; high extrapolation risk
adopt_for_our_plan: Use as early precedent that literature-extracted PLGA size/PDI/DL/EE tables can support exploratory ML figures; use cautionary example for sparse regions
do_not_adopt: Do not use size/PDI/DL-only feature space as the main design model; do not rely on GP surfaces from small sparse subsets as final evidence
field_scope_implication: Particle size, PDI, DL/LC are useful auxiliary outputs, not sufficient core design predictors. EE target alignment remains mandatory.

formulation_count: not fully explicit in extracted text; dataset constructed from over 100 research articles
paper_count: over 100 research articles using keyword PLGA NPs
molecule_count: broad PLGA NP literature; molecule count not explicit in extracted text
preparation_scope: multiple PLGA NP synthesis methods including nanoprecipitation, single/double emulsion, microfluidics
input_features_reported: 8 influential features: synthesis method; solvent 1; solvent 2; PLGA Mw; LA/GA ratio; PEG presence; PEG Mw; PVA presence
models: LASSO feature weighting/selection; SVR; RF; LR; MLP and related regression comparisons
interpretability: LASSO coefficients; model feature weights; feature-number ablations
key_limitations: coarse predictors such as PVA presence rather than concentration; limited drug descriptor use; no prospective experimental validation
adopt_for_our_plan: Use 8-feature set as minimum coarse baseline and literature support for polymer MW, LA/GA, method, solvent, PEG/PVA fields
do_not_adopt: Do not stop at binary PVA/PEG or omit drug descriptors; do not claim design validation without experiments
field_scope_implication: Tier 0/Tier 1 must include method, solvent, PLGA MW, LA/GA, surfactant/PVA, PEG indicators. More detailed concentrations should be preferred when available.

paper_count: literature-curated microfluidic PLGA formulation papers; exact count not explicit in extracted snippets
molecule_count: multiple drugs/small molecules; exact count not explicit in extracted snippets
input_features_reported: 25 key features including surfactant type; method; solvent1/2; total/aqueous/organic flow; flow ratio; PLGA/drug/surfactant/PVA concentrations; PLGA Mw; PEG%; chip type; channel diameter; PVA Mw; PEG Mw; PLGA LA/GA; size
models: RF; gradient boosting/GB; SVR; LASSO and RF feature reduction; random forest reported best with R2 about 0.96 for EE and 0.93 for DL in abstract
validation_strategy: model comparison and feature reduction; exact split details need full-method reread before citing
interpretability: RF feature importance; LASSO reduction; reduction-performance curves
key_limitations: method-specific microfluidic scope; possible optimistic performance if rows from same papers/families are randomly split; flow/channel fields not general to all PLGA methods
adopt_for_our_plan: Use as support for richer method-specific fields and tree/boosting models; use microfluidic features only when method is microfluidic
do_not_adopt: Do not make microfluidic flow/channel fields mandatory for all papers; do not compare our broader-method model directly against their microfluidic-only R2
field_scope_implication: Flow, chip, and channel fields are Tier 2/method-specific. PLGA concentration, surfactant concentration, LA/GA, PVA, organic/aqueous flow or phase ratio reinforce Tier 1/Tier 2 choices.

paper_short: Seegobin et al. 2024 International Journal of Pharmaceutics
formulation_count: 48 extreme/factorial experiments, 60 CCD, 144 factorial, 165 training data points, plus external validation set of 11 triplicate points as described in extracted text
molecule_count: not EE/drug-loading focused; production optimization context
input_features_reported: PLGA type; anti-solvent type; PLGA concentration; anti-solvent solution concentration
models: XGBoost; RF; KNN; SVM; MLP; DOE/RSM/ANOVA; random search hyperparameter optimization; 5-fold CV and LOOCV considered
validation_strategy: external unseen validation set; RMSE and R2; comparison of DOE and ML
interpretability: DOE parameter estimates/ANOVA; XGBoost feature weights
prospective_experiment: yes, experimental dataset generation and validation, but not our EE target
key_limitations: targets size/zeta, not EE; nanoprecipitation-only; small designed feature space
adopt_for_our_plan: Use as methodological support for combining DOE/ML and for 3-5 model-guided validation experiments; use XGBoost/RF/KNN/SVM/MLP comparison as candidate model family reference
do_not_adopt: Do not let size/zeta optimization replace EE target; do not require anti-solvent concentration for all methods
field_scope_implication: PLGA concentration, surfactant/anti-solvent concentration and PLGA type are high-value experimental variables. Use model-guided validation rather than full DOE for minimum paper.

paper_short: Kim/Olivetti et al. 2017 Chemistry of Materials
formulation_count: 22,065 journal articles for titania nanotube decision tree example; broader extracted synthesis corpus
paper_count: tens of thousands of materials synthesis articles
molecule_count: materials synthesis systems, not PLGA drugs
preparation_scope: materials synthesis literature, e.g. metal oxides and titania nanotubes
input_features_reported: synthesis parameters such as temperature, time, precursor/operation/condition attributes; 27 synthesis variables in titania example
targets: synthesis outcomes/morphology classes, e.g. nanotube vs not-nanotube
models: paragraph relevance classifier; NLP extraction; decision tree; logistic regression; neural extraction classifiers
validation_strategy: classification accuracies and human-annotated extraction validation; decision boundary interpretation
interpretability: decision tree rules; 2D learned synthesis maps
prospective_experiment: no direct PLGA experiment; materials insight framing
key_limitations: not PLGA; route-boundary ambiguity noted as a problem when multiple synthesis routes appear in one paper
adopt_for_our_plan: Use as literature-mining precedent and caution that formulation/route boundary matters; supports our formulation-level identity emphasis
do_not_adopt: Do not cite as PLGA field schema; do not overstate general NLP extraction accuracy for our formulation task
field_scope_implication: Provenance, route/formulation boundary, and risk flags are essential. Human audit should focus on row-boundary and target-alignment risks.

paper_count: 59 articles selected after 812 Web of Science hits
preparation_scope: nanoprecipitation / solvent displacement / interfacial deposition / solvent injection PLGA nanoparticles; small molecules; no active targeting
input_features_reported: 18 final ML-ready columns: polymer_MW; LA/GA; mol_MW; mol_logP; mol_TPSA; mol_melting_point; mol_Hacceptors; mol_Hdonors; mol_heteroatoms; drug/polymer; surfactant_concentration; surfactant_HLB; aqueous/organic; pH; solvent_polarity_index; particle_size; EE; LC
models: no primary predictive ML; distribution and Pearson correlation analysis; code from prior microparticle dataset work
validation_strategy: two independent human data collectors cross-validated discrepancies
key_limitations: nanoprecipitation-only; intentionally clean and complete manual dataset; not broader PLGA method scope; no prospective validation
adopt_for_our_plan: Use as schema anchor, external reference dataset, and proof that 433-row PLGA NP dataset is publishable/valuable; align our minimum fields with its 18 features where possible
do_not_adopt: Do not duplicate a dataset descriptor; do not restrict our novelty to manual nanoprecipitation-only curation
field_scope_implication: Tier 1 should include polymer_MW, LA/GA, drug descriptors, drug/polymer, surfactant concentration/HLB, aqueous/organic, pH, solvent polarity, EE, LC and size. Our broader methods require method class as an additional mandatory field.
